channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Judge rejects immediately restoring AP's access to White House but urges government to reconsider
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Monday refused to immediately order the White House to restore The Associated Press' access to presidential events, saying the news organization had not demonstrated it had suffered any irreparable harm.
item.dc:creator: MATT SEDENSKY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump backs Musk as he roils the federal workforce with demands and threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump backed Elon Musk's demand that federal employees explain their recent accomplishments by the end of Monday or risk getting fired, even as government agency officials were told that compliance with Musk's edict was voluntary.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inmate death reported as illegal prison guard strike stretches into 2nd week
item.description: COXSACKIE, N.Y. (AP) — A 61-year-old inmate died over the weekend at one of the New York prisons where National Guard troops were deployed to replace corrections officers who walked off the job in an illegal strike.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, CEDAR ATTANASIO and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Judge blocks 2 federal agencies from disclosing personal records to Trump adviser Musk's DOGE
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A judge agreed Monday to temporarily bar two federal agencies from disclosing records containing sensitive personal information to representatives of billionaire Trump adviser Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ron DeSantis talks up his wife as next Florida governor and takes a shot at Trump's pick
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — As Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis approaches the end of his second term in office, he’s talking up one potential heir to succeed him in 2026: his wife, Casey DeSantis. And he's taking a shot at President Donald Trump 's pick to be the next governor.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: American detained in Paris after baby is allegedly thrown from hotel window
item.description: The American was taken to a hospital for a post-delivery operation where she was placed in custody, according to the Paris Prosecutor’s Office.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Coast Guard detains 2 undocumented immigrants off Massachusetts coast
item.description: During a routine boat inspection on Thursday, the Coast Guard learned two of the six crew members were not U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: Coast Guard crews rescue three boaters stuck at sea for 9 hours after their boat capsizes
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Coast Guard crews rescued three boaters in Captiva Island, Florida, after their boat flipped over and they were stuck at sea for nine hours. A family member reported concerns at 1 a.m. Sunday after not hearing from the three boaters since 8 p.m. Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Roberta Flack, Grammy-winning ‘Killing Me Softly’ singer with an intimate style, dies at 88
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Roberta Flack, the Grammy-winning singer and pianist whose intimate vocal and musical style made her one of the top recordings artists of the 1970s and an influential performer long after, died Monday. She was 88.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump flag in Massachusetts police station gym divides town as chief, official feud over its removal: report
item.description: A Massachusetts police department chief has filed a complaint against a town administrator after he allegedly ordered the police chief to remove a Trump flag from a private gym.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump posts SpongeBob meme mocking federal workers after email requesting accomplishments
item.description: President Donald Trump posted a SpongeBob meme mocking federal employees in the wake of an email sent to the workforce asking them to document their achievements in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Come 'Beyond the Gates' for the first Black-led soap opera
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The first thing viewers of “Beyond the Gates” see is a black Mercedes gliding past manicured lawns and stately estates. The sedan pulls up at an elegant country club and the valets come out. The Emotions' funky tune “Best of My Love” is playing.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bryan Kohberger case: Idaho judge unseals transcript of closed-door IGG hearing
item.description: An Idaho judge has unsealed the transcript from a hearing where Bryan Kohberger's defense challenged investigative genetic genealogy in the student murders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 11 dead after supplemental shakes linked to listeria cases in 21 states, including Florida
item.description: Frozen shakes sold to nursing homes and other institutions are being recalled after a listeria outbreak in 21 states that has caused 11 deaths, according to the Federal Food and Drug Administration.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Johnston, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Starbucks lays off 1,100 corporate employees as coffee chain streamlines
item.description: Starbucks plans to lay off 1,100 corporate employees globally as new Chairman and CEO Brian Niccol streamlines operations. In a letter to employees released Monday, Niccol said the company will inform employees who are being laid off by mid-day Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Musk’s ultimatum deadline for federal employees hits and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Condemned South Carolina killer chooses to be executed by firing squad
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Condemned South Carolina inmate Brad Sigmon has chosen to die next month by a firing squad, a method of execution that has not been used in the U.S. in 15 years. Sigmon is scheduled to die on March 7.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek facing a deadline from Elon Musk to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces Dan Bongino as Deputy FBI Director
item.description: Bongino is a former U.S. Secret Service agent that became known for his defense of Trump while previously appearing as a Fox News contributor.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Trump administration is firing 2,000 USAID workers and putting thousands of others on leave
item.description: The Trump administration said Sunday that it was placing all but a fraction of staffers at the U.S. Agency for International Development on leave worldwide and eliminating 2,000 U.S.-based staff positions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Coast Guard, New York first responders searching for missing person after boat capsized with six passengers
item.description: A search is underway for a missing person in the water near the coast of New York after a boat capsized with six people on it, leaving three dead and two hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Avalanches in western US claim more lives in recent weeks including a weekend death in Colorado
item.description: WINTER PARK, Colo. (AP) — One person was killed in an avalanche in central Colorado on Saturday, marking just the latest fatality stemming from avalanches in the western U.S. this season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan migrants are flown home from Guantanamo Bay, with a layover in Honduras
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Nearly 200 Venezuelan immigrants to the U.S. were returned to their home country after being detained at Guantanamo Bay, in a flurry of flights that forged an unprecedented pathway for U.S. deportations. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, WGN 9

item.title: New Uber feature may overcharge you. Here's how to turn it off
item.description: Unless you disable it, a new setting in the app may end up costing you the next time you hail a ride overseas.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Hochul says she made a case to Trump for NYC's congestion pricing, yet predicts courts will decide
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York Gov. Kathy Hochul says she told President Donald Trump in a private meeting at the White House that congestion pricing tolls in New York City are necessary and working, yet the Democrat predicted the courts will likely decide the matter.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luxury portable toilets sought after ahead of NFL Draft
item.description: The luxury restroom trailers and containers feature flushing toilets, sinks, air conditioning, and even stereo systems.
item.dc:creator: Timothy Littau, KRON4

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: One dead, one injured after shooting at New Mexico Air Force base
item.description: Kirtland Air Force base said the 377th Security Forces Squadron was called to an incident at approximately 2 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Trump, Musk aren't the first to make deep cuts: Clinton-era initiative saved billions
item.description: DOGE isn't the first controversial cost-cutting push — the biggest effort to overhaul the federal government in modern history actually was 30 years ago under a Democratic administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pope Francis remains in critical condition with early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Memorial grows for Pennsylvania officer killed in hospital shooting
item.description: A memorial is growing outside the police department in West York, a borough outside of York, Pennsylvania, after officer Andrew Duarte was killed in the line of duty on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KRON4

item.title: Trump spars with Maine's governor at the White House over transgender athletes
item.description: President Donald Trump sparred with Maine's Democratic governor during a meeting of governors at the White House on Friday, with Gov. Janet Mills telling the Republican president, “We'll see you in court,” over his push to deny federal funding to the state over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, WFLA, NBC
